stimulus: At 1,991 meters, the Akashi Kaikyō Bridge in Japan is one of the longest suspension bridges in the ______blank the 1915 Çanakkale Bridge in Turkey, at 2,023 meters, is now even longer.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is the coordination of independent clauses. This choice correctly uses a comma followed by the coordinating conjunction "but" to join the first independent clause ("At 1,991 meters...world") with the second independent clause ("the 1915 Çanakkale Bridge...now even longer").

Choice A is incorrect because a comma is needed before the coordinating conjunction. Choice C is incorrect because it results in a comma splice. Choice D is incorrect because no comma is needed after "but."
